paragraph: Englewood Cliffs is a single-family market. Twenty-eight homes sold over the last eight months at a median of $1,787,500, with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.96 and a median time on market of 37 days. About 18 percent of homes sold above asking, which tells you that buyers are finding room to negotiate and sellers aren't in a position to push back hard on price. The range here is wide, from under a million to $5.4 million, which reflects the town's geography and lot sizes more than any market segmentation. Homes priced appropriately for their condition and location are moving. Homes sitting longer than that median tend to be either priced above what the market will bear or waiting for a specific buyer profile. This is a buyer-friendly environment relative to what you'd see in neighboring high-demand towns. If you're shopping in Englewood Cliffs, the lever is yours. Price your home competitively if you need to move on a timeline, or hold and wait if you can afford to find the right buyer.
